title: Strategic vs execution item fields — merged comparison
topic: Side-by-side field catalogue: portfolio items (R007) vs user stories (R008)
summary: Alphabetical merged field table showing which fields are shared, strategic-only, execution-only, or semantically divergent between portfolio items and user stories.
content:
Field Comparison

FieldStrategic item (portfolio)Execution item (user story)
acceptance_criteria⚠ Descriptions differ

Rich-text acceptance criteria for the portfolio item — the conditions that must be met for the item to be considered done. Shared field across portfolio items, user stories, and defects. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

⚠ Descriptions differ

Rich-text acceptance criteria defining the conditions that must be met for the story to be considered done. Distinct from description — this is the testable definition of done. Explicitly user-story-only in scope; portfolio items use description for equivalent purpose. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R008)

affects_doc

Flags that this story has documentation impact — product documentation must be updated as part of delivery. Used by doc teams to filter stories requiring doc work. Type: boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false

(R008)

archived

Marks the portfolio item as archived. Archived items are excluded from live queries (WHERE archived_at IS NULL) but retained indefinitely for audit. Soft-archive only — no hard deletes. Stored as archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ (NULL = live). Type: boolean (derived from archived_at IS NOT NULL)

(R007)

Marks the story as archived. Archived stories are excluded from live queries but retained for audit. Stored as archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Type: boolean (derived from archived_at IS NOT NULL)

(R008)

attachments

Files attached to this user story. Stored as pointers (storage key + metadata) in a story attachments table, not binary data in Postgres. A future story_attachments or generalised entity_attachments table covers this. Type: — (separate system)

(R008)

blocked

Indicates the item is currently blocked. Auto-set true if any direct child item is marked blocked (rollup from execution layer). Can also be set manually by the owner or padmin. Type: boolean

(R007)

Indicates the user story is currently blocked. Can be set manually by the owner or padmin. Type: boolean

(R008)

blocked_reason

Free-text explanation of why the item is blocked. Required when blocked = true is set manually; optional on rollup-derived blocks. Max recommended length 1 000 chars. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

Free-text explanation of why the story is blocked. Required when blocked = true is set manually. Conditional display: only shown when blocked = true. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R008)

count_child_defects

Total count of defect-type execution items directly parented to this portfolio item. Computed at reporting time or incrementally via trigger on child writes. Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R007)

Total count of defect-type items directly linked to this user story. Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R008)

count_child_tasks

Total count of child tasks directly parented to this user story. Computed at reporting time or incrementally. Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R008)

count_child_test_cases

Total count of test cases linked to this user story. Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R008)

count_child_user_stories

Total count of user-story-type execution items parented (directly or via leaf resolution) to this portfolio item. Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R007)

count_dependants

Count of other portfolio items or execution items that list this item as a dependency (i.e. this item blocks them). Computed from a dependency join table. Not a stored column. Type: INTEGER (computed)

(R007)

count_rollup_defect

Accepted defect count rolled up from all descendant portfolio items and their execution children — counts defects in terminal/accepted state across the full subtree. Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R007)

count_rollup_defects

Total defect count (all states) rolled up from all descendant portfolio items. Distinct from count_rollup_defect (accepted only). Type: INTEGER (computed / materialised)

(R007)

count_rollup_estimation

Total story-point estimate rolled up from all descendant execution items (all states). Used as the denominator in percent-done-by-estimate calculations. Type: NUMERIC (computed / materialised)

(R007)

count_rollup_risks

Count of risk records linked to this portfolio item and its descendants. Computed from the risk join table. Distinct from risk_score (weighted scoring). Type: INTEGER (computed)

(R007)

date_created

Timestamp at which the portfolio item row was inserted. System-generated; never editable. Maps to standard created_at column. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()

(R007)

Timestamp at which the user story row was inserted. System-generated; never editable. Maps to standard created_at column. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()

(R008)

date_updated

Timestamp of the most recent update to the user story row. Maintained by an updated_at trigger. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()

(R008)

date_work_accepted⚠ Descriptions differ

The latest date on which any child execution item moved to an Accepted/terminal state — represents when all committed work on this item was accepted. NULL if no child items have reached that state. Computed as MAX(transitioned_at) across children. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable, computed / materialised)

(R007)

⚠ Descriptions differ

The date on which this story itself moved into an Accepted / terminal schedule state. NULL if not yet accepted. Semantics differ from R007: this is the story's own state transition, not a rollup from children. Stored as a cached column updated by the state-change handler. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable, computed / materialised)

(R008)

date_work_planned_finish

User-specified target completion date. Not enforced by the system — a planning target used in roadmap views and for late-item highlighting. Type: DATE (nullable)

(R007)

date_work_planned_start

User-specified target start date. Used in roadmap / Gantt views to position the item on a timeline. Type: DATE (nullable)

(R007)

date_work_started

The earliest date on which any child execution item moved to an In-Progress (active) state — represents when delivery work actually began on this item. Computed as MIN(transitioned_at) across children. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable, computed / materialised)

(R007)

defect_status

Rolled-up status of linked defects: one of none, open, fixed, mixed. Computed from the states of all child defects. A quick-glance indicator without requiring drill-down. Type: TEXT (computed / materialised)

(R008)

description

Rich-text description of the portfolio item — its purpose, scope, and context. Supports markdown or an agreed rich-text format. Max recommended: 64 KB. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

Rich-text description of the user story — its purpose, scope, and context. Supports markdown or agreed rich-text format. Max recommended 64 KB. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R008)

discussions

Portfolio items participate in a threaded discussion system. Not a count column — a relationship to a separate discussion_posts table set. One thread per item using entity_kind discriminator. Type: — (separate system)

(R007)

User stories participate in the same threaded discussion system as portfolio items. One thread per story, using entity_kind = 'user_story'. Discussion tables from R007 Section D apply directly. Type: — (separate system)

(R008)

done_by_story_count

Percentage of child user stories in Accepted/terminal state vs. total child user stories. Computed as (accepted_story_count / total_story_count) × 100. NULL if no child stories exist. Type: NUMERIC(5,2) (computed)

(R007)

done_by_task_count

Percentage of child tasks in Accepted/terminal state vs. total child tasks. Computed as (accepted_task_count / total_task_count) × 100. NULL if no child tasks exist. Analogous to R007 done_by_story_count but for tasks. Type: NUMERIC(5,2) (computed)

(R008)

estimate_hours

Total task-level hour estimate for this story — the sum of estimated hours across all child tasks. May be entered directly or rolled up from child tasks. Type: NUMERIC(8,2) (nullable)

(R008)

estimate_initial

Initial T-shirt size estimate set during planning: one of XS, S, M, L, XL (or a configurable vocabulary). Represents the rough order-of-magnitude sizing at intake before detailed breakdown. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

estimate_points

Story point estimate for this user story — the primary planning unit for sprint capacity. Represents effort or complexity relative to other stories. NULL = unestimated. Type: NUMERIC(6,1) (nullable)

(R008)

estimate_remaining

Remaining hours of effort to complete all child tasks on this story. Updated as tasks are completed or estimates revised. Type: NUMERIC(8,2) (nullable)

(R008)

estimate_updated

Refined story-point estimate set after breakdown. Replaces estimate_initial as the delivery-planning figure once the team has enough detail. Used in rollup calculations. Type: NUMERIC(10,2) (nullable)

(R007)

expedite

Marks the story as expedited — it should be pulled ahead of normal priority ordering. Triggers visual highlighting on boards. A swimlane/WIP override signal used in Kanban teams. Type: boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false

(R008)

flow_state

The portfolio item's current position in the flow state board (Kanban column). References a row in item_type_states scoped per (subscription, item type). Transitions validated against item_type_transition_edges; history recorded in item_state_history. Type: UUID FK → item_type_states.id (nullable)

(R007)

The user story's current position in the flow state board (Kanban column). References the subscription's configured workflow states for user stories. Transitions validated against item_type_transition_edges. Flow State is distinct from Schedule State. Type: UUID FK → item_type_states.id (nullable)

(R008)

flow_state_change_owner

The user who last changed the flow state of this item. Cached alongside flow_state_change_update_date for display without joining item_state_history. Authoritative record is in item_state_history. Type: UUID FK → users.id (nullable)

(R007)

The user who last changed the flow state of this story. Cached for fast display. Authoritative record is in item_state_history. Type: UUID FK → users.id (nullable)

(R008)

flow_state_change_update_date

Timestamp of the most recent flow state transition on this item. Updated atomically with flow_state. Enables "time in current state" calculations: NOW() − flow_state_change_update_date. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable)

(R007)

Timestamp of the most recent flow state transition on this story. Updated atomically with flow_state. Enables "time in current state" calculations. Type: TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable)

(R008)

hierarchy_child

Collection of child portfolio items directly nested under this item. Not a stored column — derived from the parent_id FK on lower-level items pointing up to this one. Type: — (relationship)

(R007)

hierarchy_parent⚠ Descriptions differ

Reference to the parent portfolio item in the hierarchy. NULL means the item is at the top of its hierarchy level. Self-referencing FK within portfolio_items. ON DELETE RESTRICT. Type: UUID FK → parent portfolio item (nullable)

(R007)

⚠ Descriptions differ

Reference to the parent portfolio item (feature or lowest-level portfolio item) that this user story belongs to. NULL for stories in the flat backlog. FK target is a portfolio item, not another story. Stories cannot parent other stories in Vector. Type: UUID FK → portfolio_items.id (nullable)

(R008)

id

System-assigned unique identifier. Rendered as a human-readable tag at display time (e.g. FT-00000042) using key_num and the current tag from portfolio_item_types. The UUID never changes; the display tag is computed. Type: UUID (PK)

(R007)

System-assigned unique identifier. Rendered as a human-readable tag at display time (e.g. US-00000001) using key_num and a story prefix. The UUID never changes; the display tag is computed. Type: UUID (PK)

(R008)

lidentifier_colour

Hex colour code (e.g. #3B82F6) for the visual label badge displayed on this item in board and list views. Part of the lidentifier visual display identifier system. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

Hex colour code for the visual label badge on this story in board and list views. Same CHECK constraint as R007. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R008)

lidentifier_labels

Array of short display labels attached to the item badge (e.g. ["Strategic", "Q3"]). Displayed on the badge itself; visible on the card face. Type: TEXT[] (nullable)

(R007)

Array of short display labels attached to the story badge. Same convention as R007. Type: TEXT[] (nullable)

(R008)

lidentifier_tags

Array of metadata tags used for filtering, search, and reporting. Not necessarily displayed on the card face. Part of the lidentifier system's broader metadata layer. Type: TEXT[] (nullable)

(R007)

Array of metadata tags for filtering, search, and reporting. Same convention as R007. Type: TEXT[] (nullable)

(R008)

lidentifier_type

The portfolio item type classification — e.g. Feature, Initiative, Theme. References the subscription's portfolio_item_types catalogue. Determines the tag prefix used in the human-readable ID, the flow state workflow applied, and which custom fields are available. Type: UUID FK → portfolio_item_types.id (NOT NULL)

(R007)

name

The human-readable name of the portfolio item. Required. Must be meaningful and unique within its parent scope. Type: TEXT NOT NULL

(R007)

The human-readable name of the user story. Required. Must be meaningful and unique within its parent scope. Type: TEXT NOT NULL

(R008)

name_author

The user who created the portfolio item. System-assigned at creation time; never editable. Equivalent to "Created By" in Rally. Type: UUID FK → users.id (NOT NULL)

(R007)

The user who created the user story. System-assigned at creation time; never editable. Rally has both Created By and Submitted By; in Vector these collapse to a single creator field. Type: UUID FK → users.id (NOT NULL)

(R008)

name_owner

The user currently responsible for the portfolio item. Defaults to the creator at creation time; may be changed by padmin or the current owner. On portfolio items this is NOT NULL. Type: UUID FK → users.id (NOT NULL)

(R007)

The user currently responsible for the user story. Defaults to creator at creation; may be changed by padmin or current owner. Nullable on user stories — a story may be unassigned in the backlog. Type: UUID FK → users.id (nullable)

(R008)

notes

Free-text field for additional team decisions, implementation notes, or context that does not belong in description or acceptance_criteria. Max recommended 8 KB. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R008)

rank

LexoRank ordering key used to sort this story within its backlog or board column. Enables drag-and-drop reordering without renumbering all rows. Type: TEXT NOT NULL

(R008)

ready

Indicates the story is ready to be pulled into the next sprint or stage. A grooming signal set by the team or padmin. Mutually exclusive with blocked at the application layer. Type: boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false

(R008)

release

The release to which this user story is assigned. NULL if not yet targeted to a release. References a future releases table. Type: UUID FK → releases.id (nullable)

(R008)

risk_impact

The assessed impact level of the primary risk on this item if it materialises. Typically Low / Medium / High / Critical or a 1–5 integer. Used with risk_probability to compute risk_score. Type: TEXT or INTEGER (nullable)

(R007)

The assessed impact level if the primary risk materialises: Low / Medium / High / Critical. Optional at story level; more commonly set on portfolio items. Same CHECK vocabulary as R007. Type: TEXT or INTEGER (nullable)

(R008)

risk_probability

The assessed probability that the primary risk will occur: Low / Medium / High, or a percentage. Combined with risk_impact to produce risk_score. Type: TEXT or INTEGER (nullable)

(R007)

The assessed probability that the primary risk will occur. Same storage recommendation as R007. Type: TEXT or INTEGER (nullable)

(R008)

risk_score

Overall delivery risk assessment score for this portfolio item. May be computed from risk_impact × risk_probability or set manually by the padmin. Higher scores = higher risk. Type: NUMERIC(5,2) (nullable, computed or manually set)

(R007)

Overall delivery risk assessment score for this user story. May be set manually or derived from linked risk records. Full risk detail lives in a linked risks table. Type: NUMERIC(5,2) (nullable)

(R008)

schedule_state

The coarse-grained lifecycle state of the user story: one of defined, in_progress, completed, accepted. Distinct from flow_state (Kanban column). Used for backlog filtering and release reporting. Type: TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'defined'

(R008)

sprint

The sprint or iteration to which this user story is assigned. NULL for unscheduled backlog stories. References a future iterations table. Type: UUID FK → iterations.id (nullable)

(R008)

strategic_investment_group

The investment area or strategic bucket this item belongs to — e.g. "Growth", "User Reach", "Efficiency", "Compliance". Used to group items by investment theme in portfolio views and reporting. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

strategic_investment_weight

The relative weight or priority of this item within its strategic_investment_group: Low / Medium / High. Used to scale items within a group for prioritisation and resource allocation views. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

strategic_item_type

Classification of the nature of the investment or work this item represents — e.g. "New Capability", "Technical Debt", "Compliance", "Maintenance". Complements the strategic investment group with a type-of-work dimension. Type: TEXT (nullable)

(R007)

test_case_status

Rollup status of linked test case results: one of none, passed, failed, mixed. Computed from the most recent execution results of all linked test cases. Type: TEXT (computed / materialised)

(R008)

value_stream_identifier

Associates the portfolio item with a value stream — the end-to-end flow of value delivery from concept to customer. Used for value stream mapping and SAFe-aligned views. Type: TEXT or UUID FK (nullable)

(R007)

Notes

Shared fields (identical or near-identical semantics): 20 — archived, blocked, blocked_reason, count_child_defects, date_created, description, discussions, flow_state, flow_state_change_owner, flow_state_change_update_date, id, lidentifier_colour, lidentifier_labels, lidentifier_tags, name, name_author, risk_impact, risk_probability, risk_score. Additionally, name_owner is shared in name and intent but has a nullability difference (NOT NULL on portfolio items, nullable on user stories) — treated as near-identical with a nuance noted in each cell.

Fields with divergent semantics (same name, meaningfully different behaviour): 4 — acceptance_criteria (shared name in R007 but R008 explicitly scopes it as story-only; portfolio use is debated), date_work_accepted (rollup from children on portfolio vs. own state transition on story), hierarchy_parent (self-referencing within portfolio_items vs. FK to portfolio_items from user_stories), name_owner (NOT NULL on portfolio, nullable on story).

Strategic-only fields (portfolio items, not applicable to user stories): 18 — count_child_user_stories, count_dependants, count_rollup_defect, count_rollup_defects, count_rollup_estimation, count_rollup_risks, date_work_planned_finish, date_work_planned_start, date_work_started, done_by_story_count, estimate_initial, estimate_updated, hierarchy_child, lidentifier_type, strategic_investment_group, strategic_investment_weight, strategic_item_type, value_stream_identifier.

Execution-only fields (user stories, not applicable to portfolio items): 17 — affects_doc, attachments, count_child_tasks, count_child_test_cases, date_updated, defect_status, done_by_task_count, estimate_hours, estimate_points, estimate_remaining, expedite, notes, rank, ready, release, schedule_state, sprint, test_case_status.
